description_long: This is a twin-engine, propeller-driven airplane with a high-wing configuration and a conventional tail. The fuselage is primarily light gray with orange accents on the nose, wingtips, and tail, and it features a forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows. The wings are straight and extend laterally from the upper section of the fuselage, with no visible winglets. The landing gear is tricycle-type, with two main gear units under the wings and a single nose gear, all of which appear to be fixed. The engines are mounted on the wings, each with a multi-blade propeller. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, forming a classic tail design.
description: A twin-propeller, high-wing airplane with a gray fuselage, orange accents, and a conventional tail. It features a forward, enclosed cockpit and fixed tricycle landing gear.
description_view_0: Side view showing the high-mounted wings, twin propeller engines, and orange accents on the nose, wingtips, and tail.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the straight wings, twin engines, and the single vertical stabilizer of the tail.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same high-wing, twin-engine configuration and color scheme as view 0.
description_view_3: Front view emphasizing the forward cockpit, propeller engines, and the broad wingspan with orange-tipped wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin turboprop engines mounted on the wings.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main gear units and a single nose gear, all fixed.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
